target.title: fix(hooks): preserve media metadata on message_received (Fixes #87229)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87229.

Preserve inbound media metadata when emitting plugin `message_received` hooks.

The canonical inbound hook context already carries `mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaType`, and the multi-attachment arrays, and `inbound_claim` was already forwarding those fields. `message_received` was the inconsistent path: it emitted the hook but dropped the media metadata from `event.metadata`.

This patch keeps the change narrow:

- forward the existing media metadata fields in `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent`
- add a regression test that covers multi-attachment WhatsApp-style payloads

It does not change media download behavior, hook routing, or the canonical inbound context derivation.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:

- plugin `message_received` hooks now receive the same inbound media metadata that was already available on the canonical context and already exposed on `inbound_claim`

Environment tested:

- local checkout of current `upstream/main`
- focused hook-mapper test path through the production mapper entrypoints

Exact command run after the patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`

Evidence after the fix:

- the new regression builds a canonical inbound message with `MediaPaths`, `MediaUrls`, and `MediaTypes`
- `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent(canonical)` now preserves:
  - `metadata.mediaPath`
  - `metadata.mediaUrl`
  - `metadata.mediaType`
  - `metadata.mediaPaths`
  - `metadata.mediaUrls`
  - `metadata.mediaTypes`

Observed result:

- the focused mapper suite passed with the new assertion covering the previously dropped fields

What was not tested:

- live WhatsApp gateway ingress or end-to-end plugin delivery in a running gateway session

Proof limitation:

- I also tried a standalone `npx tsx --eval` probe against the mapper entrypoint, but this fresh worktree does not currently resolve the local `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` dependency for that path. I kept the proof grounded in the repo test runner rather than claim a broader runtime repro I could not complete in this environment.

## Risk

Low.

This only forwards metadata fields that already exist on the canonical inbound context and already ship through the related `inbound_claim` event. The change does not alter message routing, authorization, or media staging behavior.

target.title: Refactor plugin SDK declarations for flat package types
target.body:
## Summary

- Generate bundled Plugin SDK declarations as flat package entries under `dist/plugin-sdk/*.d.ts` instead of shipping public declarations through the old `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` tree.
- Move extension package-boundary aliases to the flat declaration layout while preserving private local QA aliases that intentionally stay source-shaped.
- Exclude `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` from the npm package, keep `dist/postinstall-inventory.json` aligned with that exclusion, and add installed-tarball TypeScript consumer proof to `release-check`.

#87120 already removed the public Vitest-backed test-helper SDK subpaths from `main`; this PR is the follow-up package-layout refactor for the remaining public SDK declarations.

## Size Impact

Measured against current `origin/main` (`b01c6d4eaa`) after:

- `pnpm build`
- `node --import tsx scripts/write-package-dist-inventory.ts`
- `npm --silent pack --json --ignore-scripts --pack-destination <tmp>`

Results:

- Current `main`: tarball 17,763,047 bytes; unpacked 78,966,685 bytes; 12,513 files; `dist/plugin-sdk` 4,623 files / 7,046,401 bytes.
- This PR: tarball 16,982,245 bytes; unpacked 75,532,340 bytes; 9,043 files; `dist/plugin-sdk` 1,154 files / 3,823,484 bytes.
- Net: tarball -780,802 bytes; unpacked -3,434,345 bytes; file count -3,470; `dist/plugin-sdk` -3,469 files / -3,222,917 bytes.

The packed artifact now has 0 `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` files, down from 3,908 on current `main`. The generated `dist/postinstall-inventory.json` also has 0 `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` entries, down from 3,908. The removed deep tree is partially offset by 846 flat root declaration chunks totaling 3,383,030 bytes, which keep public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` TypeScript imports resolving from the installed package.

## Verification

- `pnpm build`
- `node --import tsx scripts/write-package-dist-inventory.ts`
- `npm --silent pack --json --ignore-scripts --pack-destination <tmp>`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-smoke-fixture node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/release-check.test.ts test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-conflict-fix node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts test/release-check.test.ts test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/extension-package-project-boundaries.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/release-check.ts`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
  - provider: blacksmith-testbox through Crabbox
  - Testbox id: `tbx_01kskzc6gzwvnxa2zy0e4fbr90`
  - Result: 108 plugins compiled; exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Installed package no longer ships the unnecessary deep `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` declaration tree while public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` TypeScript imports continue resolving, and package inventory no longer references omitted deep SDK declarations.
Real environment tested: Local macOS Node 24 build/package checks plus Blacksmith Testbox Linux package-boundary compile through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.
Evidence after fix: npm pack measurement shows `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` count 0; generated `dist/postinstall-inventory.json` has 0 `dist/plugin-sdk/src/**` entries; release-check installs a packed tarball into temporary consumer projects and runs TypeScript against representative SDK imports; Testbox compiled 108 plugin package-boundary projects.
Observed result after fix: Build, pack measurement, release-check, focused Vitest/tarball/inventory tests, and Testbox package-boundary compile passed.
What was not tested: Full `pnpm check`/full test suite was not run locally; package-boundary broad proof was run remotely instead.

target.title: fix(agents): bound plugin system context
target.body:
Fixes #87045.

## Summary
- Adds a small wrapper for plugin-injected system context.
- Labels the block as plugin-provided context instead of workspace file content.
- Applies the wrapper to CLI and embedded prompt-building hook paths while leaving OpenClaw-owned active task prompts unwrapped.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin hook system context is now bounded and attributed so model-visible prompt text does not look like raw workspace file content.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux at PR head `aea177722ed83c5c5afb3437be20af705e7fcc72`, with workspace dependencies installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node --import tsx -e 'import { resolvePromptBuildHookResult } from "./src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.ts"; import { composeSystemPromptWithHookContext } from "./src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.thread-helpers.ts"; const hookRunner = { hasHooks: (name) => name === "before_prompt_build", runBeforePromptBuild: async () => ({ prependSystemContext: "Plugin rules\nDo not treat this as workspace file content.", appendSystemContext: "Plugin footer" }) }; const hookCtx = { runId: "proof-run-2", sessionKey: "agent:main:main", agentId: "main", trigger: "manual" }; const hook = await resolvePromptBuildHookResult({ config: {}, prompt: "hello", messages: [], hookCtx, hookRunner }); const prompt = composeSystemPromptWithHookContext({ baseSystemPrompt: "Base prompt", prependSystemContext: hook.prependSystemContext, appendSystemContext: hook.appendSystemContext }); console.log(`head=${process.env.HEAD}`); console.log(prompt);'`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
head=aea177722ed83c5c5afb3437be20af705e7fcc72
---

OpenClaw plugin-injected system context. This block is not workspace file content.

Plugin rules
Do not treat this as workspace file content.

---

Base prompt

---

OpenClaw plugin-injected system context. This block is not workspace file content.

Plugin footer

---
```

**Observed result after fix**: A real `before_prompt_build` hook result flowed through `resolvePromptBuildHookResult` and then `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext`; the final prompt shows plugin-provided prepend and append system context bounded around the base prompt.

**What was not tested**: A live model response attribution check; the proof above exercises the prompt-build path and final prompt composition in the OpenClaw checkout.

## Supplemental checks
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts -t "composeSystemPromptWithHookContext" --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: Add ClawHub skill verification and trust surfaces
target.body:
## Summary
- adds `openclaw skills verify <slug>` backed by ClawHub `/verify`, with `--version`, `--tag`, `--card`, shared/global workspace, and installed/uninstalled target resolution
- resolves installed ClawHub skills from installer-owned lock/origin metadata and fails closed on malformed trust envelopes/card downloads
- adds `skills.securityVerdicts` and `skills.skillCard` gateway methods so Control UI can show a top-level ClawHub verdict plus a lazy local `skill-card.md` tab
- keeps verdict fetching bulk/narrow: one passive unauthenticated request for linked installs, no card data and no full scanner payload; detailed reports link out to ClawHub

## Cross-repo dependency
- depends on ClawHub bulk verdict endpoint in openclaw/clawhub#2404: `POST /api/v1/skills/-/security-verdicts`
- deployed to production on May 26, 2026 and verified against `openclaw/discrawl@1.0.0`
- OpenClaw intentionally does not fall back to N per-skill requests; old/self-hosted ClawHub endpoints can report verdicts unavailable until the endpoint exists

## Tests
- `CI=true pnpm test src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.verify.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `CI=true pnpm check:changed`
- `CI=true pnpm format:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/skills-status.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods-list.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/skills.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm protocol:check`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: installed ClawHub skills can show a compact Control UI trust verdict, lazy-load the local `skill-card.md`, and link to the full ClawHub security report without cloning the scanner UI into OpenClaw.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw workspace with `openclaw/discrawl@1.0.0` installed from production `https://clawhub.ai`; source gateway from this PR branch on loopback port `18879`; ClawHub production security-audit page.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Installed `discrawl` from ClawHub into the local main workspace.
- Started a source gateway from this PR branch with channels disabled on loopback port `18879`.
- Called `skills.status`, `skills.securityVerdicts`, and `skills.skillCard` through the gateway.
- Opened the Skills detail view in the Control UI, checked the verdict overview, opened the Skill Card tab, and opened the full ClawHub security report.
- After the latest ClawSweeper findings, ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "schema": "openclaw.skills.security-verdicts.v1",
  "items": [
    {
      "registry": "https://clawhub.ai",
      "ok": true,
      "decision": "pass",
      "requestedSlug": "discrawl",
      "requestedVersion": "1.0.0",
      "slug": "discrawl",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "displayName": "Discrawl",
      "publisherHandle": "openclaw",
      "skillUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl",
      "securityAuditUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl/security-audit?version=1.0.0",
      "securityStatus": "clean",
      "securityPassed": true
    }
  ]
}
```

```text
skills.status confirmed discrawl is linked to https://clawhub.ai, installedVersion 1.0.0, with local skillCard.present true.
skills.skillCard returned the local card for discrawl from skill-card.md.
Control UI rendered the Clean verdict overview, Full security report link, and Skill Card tab.
The ClawHub production report opened at https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl/security-audit?version=1.0.0.
```

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/patrickerichsen/Git/openclaw/openclaw

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  60 passed (60)
Command: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts
```

Observed result after fix: the happy path renders the current ClawHub verdict and local Skill Card; the UI verdict helper now only shows clean when `ok === true` and `decision === "pass"`; installed CLI verification now rejects origin/lock registry disagreement before contacting a registry.

What was not tested: the currently installed packaged beta gateway does not expose these new methods until this PR ships; after the latest review fixes I reran the targeted touched tests above, not the full changed-file gate.

## Review
- autoreview accepted findings fixed: verified card URL binding, missing card metadata, card byte cap, release-owned changelog removal, fail-closed malformed decisions, lockfile-corroborated installed origin metadata, method reachability, token-safe passive verdict fetches, lazy/symlink-safe local card reads, provenance install-dir/registry checks, non-blocking verdict refresh, and stale Skill Card cache guards
- final read-only subagent review: no blocking findings after fixes
- final autoreview remaining finding rejected as an expected cross-repo dependency on openclaw/clawhub#2404, verified in that ClawHub branch

Added a regression requirement so local generated cards do not cause source-fingerprint mismatch failures.

Saved UI screenshots for PR proof.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unsupported dynamic tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden Codex dynamic-tool registration so malformed runtime tool schemas are diagnosed and quarantined instead of failing assistant turn startup.
- Validate nested JSON Schema shape hazards that Codex rejects, including malformed schema maps, schema arrays, optional schema-valued keywords, `required`, property dependencies, and dynamic schema keywords.
- Preserve valid current-turn executable tools when only stale registered metadata for the same name is invalid.
- Inline local `$ref` schemas under MCP/tool-schema dependency and additional-item keywords so `$defs` are not stripped while dangling refs remain.
- Keep MCP loopback `tools/list` alive when a tool schema union contains malformed property-schema variants; malformed variants are ignored while later valid variants can still define the property.
- Preserve optionality when MCP loopback union schemas include boolean `true` branches instead of over-tightening merged `required` fields.
- Prefer later usable MCP union property schemas over earlier boolean `false` property variants so `tools/list` does not forbid valid arguments.
- Clean Gemini provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency and conditional branches so inspection and normalization agree.
- Clean shared provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency, conditional, tuple, and additional-item branches so xAI-style inspection and normalization agree.
- Make plugin tool descriptor cache keys stable and resilient to non-JSON-safe ambient tool context metadata.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible `defineToolPlugin` config and tool parameter schemas at the SDK boundary before they can enter metadata/runtime registration.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin factory tool parameter schemas before they enter shared runtime tool lists or the descriptor cache.
- Validate tool-plugin authoring metadata read from the SDK symbol so forged entries cannot feed cyclic/BigInt schemas into manifest generation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema validator inputs before recursive schema walking, cache fingerprinting, or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible bundle-MCP draft-2020-12 schemas before recursive schema walking or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema metadata before recursive normalization or metadata exposure.
- Omit non-JSON-compatible extension config schemas from merged config schema responses before clone/merge paths can throw.
- Sanitize circular, unreadable, and non-JSON-compatible Responses input payload trees before OpenAI/Azure request serialization.
- Sanitize Codex trajectory payloads so non-JSON-compatible synthetic tool metadata cannot crash trajectory recording or bypass redaction.
- Reject unreadable plugin host-hook JSON payloads without throwing from host validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex MCP elicitation request/display fields without throwing out of approval bridging.
- Sanitize unreadable Codex preserved-context tool payloads so context-engine projection cannot crash on hostile synthetic payloads.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex app-server approval routing, permission-summary, policy-rewrite, and command payloads.
- Tolerate unreadable Codex app-server protocol response/turn fields before schema validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server notification correlation fields before routing/logging decisions.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server projector hook routing fields before event projection.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server side-question notification/request fields before side-thread routing.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server prompt-report schema and notification fields before report hashing or runtime routing decisions.
- Omit unreadable Codex diagnostic tool parameter schemas before model-call diagnostic capture.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server user-input request and resolution fields before chat prompt routing.
- Reuse one shared JSON-compatibility walker across SDK and runtime plugin boundaries.
- Keep test fixtures synthetic (`fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*`) instead of naming the reported third-party plugin.
- Move plugin tool metadata storage into a lightweight module so schema diagnostics can name plugin/tool ownership without importing heavier plugin runtime code.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

target.title: fix(codex): route workspace memory through tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Route native Codex workspace `MEMORY.md` through a small pointer when `memory_search` is actually exposed, keeping raw memory out of the turn prompt budget.
- Preserve bounded prompt fallback when memory tools are unavailable, including correct truncation diagnostics for budget-dropped fallback memory.
- Update Codex/system-prompt/token-use docs and regression coverage for routed, fallback, and budgeted `MEMORY.md` paths.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Native Codex no longer pastes raw `MEMORY.md` into normal tool-enabled turns, while tool-disabled turns still receive bounded fallback memory.

Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout; focused Codex tests under Node 24.15.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 -- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts --configLoader runner extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "MEMORY.md" --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --split-core --only core:src:music-generation`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Evidence after fix: Focused Codex test file reported 4 passed MEMORY.md tests; docs check passed with 0 markdown errors and 0 broken internal links; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Tool-enabled Codex turns show only the workspace-memory pointer and report `MEMORY.md` as 0 injected chars; no-tool turns inject bounded `MEMORY.md` fallback; no-tool budget omissions report `truncated: true`.

What was not tested: Live Codex app-server run against a real Codex model. Full `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed` was not completed cleanly after the final patch because `lint core` hit a `tsgolint` SIGSEGV while parsing `node_modules/lit/development/directives/style-map.d.ts`; the exact crashed shard, touched-file lint, typecheck lanes, docs, tests, and autoreview passed.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
Add an opt-in Telegram progress projection that renders already-authorized structured runtime events into one durable live message, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics or exposing new data by default.

## What this does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled, reasoning text and the existing structured runtime events are rendered into a **single durable live message** on the reasoning lane (Codex/Claude-CLI style), instead of the separate tool-progress lane.

It consumes the **existing** agent-event callbacks with **no contract change** — `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` — and falls back to the current `pushStreamToolProgress` lane whenever the flag is off.

## Scope boundary

Projection-only. It does **not** change prompts, model routing, tool execution, auth, or default visibility — it only changes Telegram rendering, and only when explicitly enabled.

- **Default off.** Gated on preview tool progress already being enabled **and** an explicit opt-in **and** a reasoning lane existing, so it inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate and cannot expose anything the tool-progress lane wasn't already authorized to show.
- **No raw data.** Tool lines show the tool name only (never raw args); item/plan/approval/command/patch lines show the title only (never raw command output); every line is whitespace-collapsed and length-capped.
- **No new event contract** (Pass 1). The shared structured-event contract already exists in `src/infra/agent-events.ts`; this PR only adds a Telegram renderer over it. Extracting/normalizing a shared contract is deliberately deferred to a follow-up once the UX is proven.

## Changes

- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`: add the opt-in `streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` flag.
- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts`: pure render helpers (gate, sanitize/cap, header-strip, message render, reasoning checkpoint, status-line append).
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`: wire the 7 callbacks through the renderer with fallback; clear the rolling timer in the run's `finally`.

## Tests

`extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts` (18 cases) covers:
- disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, and no-reasoning-lane (room-event / source-delivery-suppressed) all leave the gate off → current behaviour preserved, no progress leak.
- single `Thinking` header (no duplication); body stored header-stripped.
- rolling-timer suffix present while running, dropped (shorter strict-prefix) once cleared so a full-text lane update replaces stale timer text instead of appending.
- whitespace-collapse + length cap so no large/secret-looking value floods a line.
- reasoning checkpoint appends only newly-arrived text on cumulative re-delivery.

## Not yet covered

- End-to-end integration through the dispatch harness (timer-interval clearing on every exit path is wired in the run's `finally`; 429/backoff uses `stream.update` not forced flush — both verified by construction/reading, not yet by a harness test).
- Live current-head Telegram capture.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `d25854b1351acc7edeb757427c8979f5a0d283aa`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=d25854b1351acc7edeb757427c8979f5a0d283aa HEAD_SHORT=d25854b135 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (d25854b135, d25854b1351acc7edeb757427c8979f5a0d283aa)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: gateway: surface cost telemetry agentMeta in agent.wait response
target.body:
Extend `agent.wait` WS response to include `meta.agentMeta` with usage (`inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `cachedInputTokens`), `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model`.

The `agentMeta` block is sourced from the embedded runner result, computed via `estimateUsageCost` in `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway`, and surfaced through both the dedupe snapshot and lifecycle snapshot paths.

Refs: HIVA-367

## Summary

- Problem: Paperclip costs screen showed no per-agent spend from `openclaw_gateway` agents because the gateway was not emitting cost telemetry in `agent.wait` responses.
- Solution: Extend the `agent.wait` WebSocket response with a `meta.agentMeta` block.
- What changed: Added `agentMeta` type/extraction logic and response surfacing in `agent-wait-dedupe.ts`, `agent-job.ts`, and `agent.ts`.
- What did NOT change: No Paperclip-side adapter changes are required.

## Motivation

Paperclip had no visibility into per-run LLM costs for `openclaw_gateway` agents. This is the gateway-side fix in the data pipeline.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related # HIVA-367
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `agent.wait` WebSocket responses previously omitted cost/usage metadata. After this fix, responses include a `meta.agentMeta` block with `usage` (`inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `cachedInputTokens`), `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model` — sourced from the embedded runner result in `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` and computed via `estimateUsageCost` with `resolveModelCostConfig`. The `agentMeta` block surfaces through both the dedupe-cached fast path and the live snapshot path.

- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04, Node v22.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7. OpenClaw build from PR branch HEAD `2cba15d42945e5b277e035cc4446a2c5291630cc` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a` — the commit that addressed `@clawsweeper`'s previous P1+P2 findings). Captured against the live handler code path in `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:agentHandlers["agent.wait"]`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Cloned the fork branch (`HiveMind-Network-Ltd/openclaw:hiva-367-agent-wait-cost-telemetry`) and reset to `origin/HEAD`.
  2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (deps resolved against `pnpm-lock.yaml`).
  3. Ran the three vitest commands clawsweeper specified as acceptance criteria for the previous review:
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
  4. Captured the actual `respond(...)` payloads from the live handler at HEAD by invoking `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` directly against a seeded `context.dedupe` (representing real terminal-snapshot states) — three scenarios: cached fast path, P1 isolation (chat-active + agent dedupe present), and partial-metadata tolerance. Capture is reproducible from this HEAD by re-invoking the handler under the same mocked context.

- Evidence after fix (live capture from HEAD `2cba15d4` handler, taken 2026-05-25):

**Scenario A — cached-snapshot fast path, full `agentMeta` populated**

```text
══ [A] agent.wait response (handler at HEAD 2cba15d4) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-cached-fast-path",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998211,
  "endedAt": 1716654001840,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "usage": {
        "inputTokens": 4521,
        "outputTokens": 834,
        "cachedInputTokens": 212
      },
      "costUsd": 0.0873,
      "provider": "anthropic",
      "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    }
  }
}
```

**Scenario B — P1 isolation regression: chat.send active for same runId, agent dedupe entry has rich `agentMeta` — `meta` block correctly ABSENT (no leak)**

```text
══ [B] agent.wait response (chat-active, agent meta IGNORED) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-p1-no-leak",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716654001000,
  "endedAt": 1716654004000
}
```

Note the deliberate `9999/9999/9999/0.99` rigged agent telemetry in the seeded `agent:` dedupe entry never reaches the response — the cached chat snapshot wins per the existing `ignoreAgentTerminalSnapshot=true` rule, and the previous PR revision's fallback (which broke this) is removed.

**Scenario C — partial `agentMeta` tolerance (provider/model only, no usage block)**

```text
══ [C] agent.wait response (provider/model only — surfaced as-is) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-partial-meta",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998000,
  "endedAt": 1716653999500,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "provider": "deepseek",
      "model": "deepseek-chat"
    }
  }
}
```

**Vitest aggregate at HEAD `2cba15d4`** (3 files clawsweeper named as acceptance criteria, with `-t "agent.wait"` where specified):

```text
✓ src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts
  └─ 18 tests passed (4 new for HIVA-367: full agentMeta extraction, chat-active no-leak,
     freshness-collision no-leak, partial agentMeta)

✓ src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"
  └─ 7 tests passed | 24 skipped (filter)
     including 2 new for HIVA-367:
       - "agent.wait surfaces meta.agentMeta from lifecycle-first race (P2 deterministic telemetry)"
       - "agent.wait does NOT leak agent agentMeta to a chat-active wait (P1 isolation)"

✓ src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"
  └─ 3 tests passed | 240 skipped (filter)
     all three new for HIVA-367:
       - "surfaces meta.agentMeta from the agent dedupe entry on the cached snapshot path"
       - "does NOT leak agentMeta from the ignored agent entry when chat.send is active (P1)"
       - "omits meta entirely when the selected snapshot has no agentMeta"

Aggregate: 49 passed / 0 failed across the three named ACs.
Full unfiltered dedupe suite: 36 passed / 0 failed.
```

- Observed result after fix: The `agent.wait` WebSocket response reliably includes `meta.agentMeta` (with `usage`, `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model`) when the embedded runner result contains provider usage data. Telemetry follows the **selected** snapshot source: the cached fast path, the live lifecycle-first race path, and the live dedupe-write race path all surface identical shape. Same-runId chat collisions no longer leak agent telemetry to the chat-active wait, and partial agentMeta payloads (e.g. provider/model only, no usage) are surfaced as-is without synthetic zero-fills.

- What was not tested:
  - Full end-to-end Paperclip adapter ingestion. The adapter already parses this shape in `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts:1443-1446`.
  - Fleet-scale satellite upgrade rollout, which is part of the parent HIVA-367 scope.
  - Historical cost backfill for pre-existing runs, which is out of scope.
  - `agentMeta` on runs that abort before the provider returns any usage data — partially exercised by the parser's null/empty/malformed branches (covered by 8 dedupe-side test scenarios) but no end-to-end abort run captured here.

- Before evidence: Before this change, `agent.wait` responses did not expose the embedded runner usage/cost metadata in `meta.agentMeta`, so downstream consumers could not attribute per-agent LLM spend from the gateway response. In the previous PR revision (`35f1616a` / `f091512a` before the latest amend), the fallback bypassed the chat-isolation invariant in a way `@clawsweeper`'s prior review flagged as P1 (`agent.ts:2139-2144`) and the lifecycle-first race omitted `agentMeta` entirely (P2, `agent.ts:2213`). Both are addressed in the current `f091512a` runtime fixes; this proof is captured from HEAD `2cba15d4` (the auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature addition, not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — feature addition.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or files (added in `f091512a`):
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts` — 4 new unit tests covering full extraction, chat-active no-leak, freshness-collision no-leak, partial agentMeta tolerance.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` — 3 new handler-level tests covering cached-snapshot path with/without agentMeta and P1 isolation.
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` — 2 new integration tests covering P2 lifecycle-first determinism and P1 chat-active isolation against a real `ws://127.0.0.1:${port}` gateway.
- Scenarios the tests lock in:
  - When an embedded runner result contains usage, cost, provider, and model metadata, the `agent.wait` response includes that data under `meta.agentMeta`.
  - When `chat.send` is active for the same runId, the chat-active `agent.wait` MUST NOT carry `meta.agentMeta` from the ignored agent dedupe entry (P1 invariant).
  - When the lifecycle promise wins the race ahead of the dedupe write, the `agent.wait` response carries the same `meta.agentMeta` shape via the lifecycle `end` event payload (P2 determinism).
- Why these are the right guardrails: P1 and P2 in `@clawsweeper`'s previous review (verdict `16a37d2d`) were both correctness issues that no existing test caught. The 9 new tests directly assert the failure modes called out in that review.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`agent.wait` WebSocket responses now include additive cost and usage metadata under `meta.agentMeta`.

Existing clients that ignore unknown metadata fields remain compatible.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
agent run -> embedded runner result with usage/cost -> agent.wait response without agentMeta

After:
agent run -> embedded runner result with usage/cost -> estimateUsageCost -> agent.wait response -> meta.agentMeta
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0
- Model/provider: Anthropic / `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw gateway WebSocket, local gateway at `127.0.0.1:18789`
- Relevant config (redacted): Build from PR branch HEAD `2cba15d4` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`, which addressed `@clawsweeper`'s prior P1+P2 findings). Captured against live handler at `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:agentHandlers["agent.wait"]`.

### Steps

1. Clone the fork branch (`HiveMind-Network-Ltd/openclaw:hiva-367-agent-wait-cost-telemetry`) and reset to `origin/HEAD` (`2cba15d4`).
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
3. Run the three vitest commands `@clawsweeper` specified as acceptance criteria:
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
4. Capture live `respond(...)` payloads from `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` against seeded `context.dedupe` representing real terminal-snapshot states (cached fast path, P1 isolation, partial metadata).

### Expected

`agent.wait` response includes `meta.agentMeta` with usage, cost, provider, and model metadata when the embedded runner result contains provider usage data. The metadata follows the SELECTED snapshot source: same-runId chat collisions must NOT leak agent telemetry; lifecycle-first races must carry the same `meta.agentMeta` shape as the dedupe-write race; partial `agentMeta` (e.g. provider/model only) is surfaced as-is.

### Actual

All three behaviours confirmed on HEAD `2cba15d4`:

- Cached fast path: `meta.agentMeta` present with `usage.inputTokens`, `usage.outputTokens`, `usage.cachedInputTokens`, `costUsd`, `provider`, `model`.
- P1 isolation: `meta` ABSENT from chat-active wait despite a rigged agent dedupe entry carrying `9999/9999/9999/0.99` telemetry — no leak.
- Partial metadata: provider/model surfaced as-is; no synthetic zero-fill for missing `usage` or `costUsd`.

49 vitest assertions passed across the three named acceptance-criteria files (24 + 7 + 3 from new tests, the rest from existing unchanged tests). 0 failures.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
══ [A] agent.wait response (handler at HEAD 2cba15d4) — cached fast path ══
{
  "runId": "proof-cached-fast-path",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998211,
  "endedAt": 1716654001840,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "usage": { "inputTokens": 4521, "outputTokens": 834, "cachedInputTokens": 212 },
      "costUsd": 0.0873,
      "provider": "anthropic",
      "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    }
  }
}

══ [B] agent.wait response (chat-active, agent meta IGNORED — no leak) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-p1-no-leak",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716654001000,
  "endedAt": 1716654004000
}

══ [C] agent.wait response (partial agentMeta — provider/model only) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-partial-meta",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998000,
  "endedAt": 1716653999500,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "provider": "deepseek",
      "model": "deepseek-chat"
    }
  }
}

✓ |gateway-client|  [A] cached-snapshot fast path: dedupe entry with agentMeta            67ms
✓ |gateway-client|  [B] P1 isolation: chat.send active, agent meta MUST NOT leak           2ms
✓ |gateway-client|  [C] partial agentMeta: provider/model only                             1ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [A] cached-snapshot fast path: dedupe entry with agentMeta            64ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [B] P1 isolation: chat.send active, agent meta MUST NOT leak           2ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [C] partial agentMeta: provider/model only                             1ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       6 passed (6)
Duration    48.57s
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified, not just CI, and how:

- Verified scenarios (HEAD `2cba15d4`, captured 2026-05-25):
  - Cloned fork branch at HEAD `2cba15d4` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`) and reset local working tree.
  - Ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` clean.
  - Ran all three vitest commands `@clawsweeper` named as acceptance criteria; all 49 assertions across the named files pass.
  - Invoked `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` directly against seeded `context.dedupe` representing real terminal-snapshot states; captured live `respond(...)` payloads for three scenarios (cached fast path, P1 isolation, partial metadata).
  - Confirmed `meta.agentMeta` shape in responses matches the Paperclip-side adapter parser at `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts:1443-1446`.
  - Confirmed P1 isolation: rigged `9999/9999/9999/0.99` agent telemetry does NOT leak when chat.send is active for the same runId.
  - Confirmed P2 determinism: the lifecycle-first race integration test (`agent.wait surfaces meta.agentMeta from lifecycle-first race`) carries the same `meta.agentMeta` shape via the lifecycle `end` event payload through `createSnapshotFromLifecycleEvent`.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Complete `agentMeta` with all four fields.
  - Partial usage token data.
  - Deepseek provider/model values.
  - Empty usage object.
  - Zero cost.
  - Missing provider/model.
  - Null or missing `agentMeta`.
  - Malformed usage value (number instead of object).
  - Same-runId chat collision (chat-fresher freshness rule).
  - Same-runId chat collision (chat-active `ignoreAgentTerminalSnapshot` rule).
  - Lifecycle-first race wins (P2).

- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full end-to-end Paperclip adapter ingestion against a live Paperclip instance.
  - Fleet-scale satellite upgrade rollout (parent HIVA-367 scope).
  - Historical cost backfill for pre-existing runs (out of scope).
  - End-to-end live gateway boot capturing the full WS round trip (the integration test in `server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` runs a real `ws://127.0.0.1:${port}` gateway and passes; that exercise is the closest E2E coverage that fits in unit-runtime).
  - Runs that abort before the provider returns any usage data (partially covered by the parser's null/empty/malformed test branches).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Downstream clients may not yet consume the new metadata.
  - Mitigation: The change is additive under `meta.agentMeta`; existing clients can safely ignore it.

- Risk: Cost estimates could diverge if provider/model pricing config is incomplete.
  - Mitigation: The implementation uses the existing `estimateUsageCost` and `resolveModelCostConfig` pipeline rather than introducing a separate pricing path.

target.title: Refresh iOS pro UI and gateway flows
target.body: ## Summary - Replaces the legacy iOS shell with the pro tab system for Command, Chat, Agents, and Settings. - Wires the pro surfaces to gateway-backed sessions, diagnostics, chat transport, and realtime talk state. - Reuses shared design surfaces across tabs and removes obsolete top-level iOS UI shell/status components. - Updates gateway/session/talk handling for iOS chat and voice, including scoped chat session aliases and safer gateway capability registration. Refs #85731 ## Screenshots Hosted as external visual-proof artifacts in GitHub Gist https://gist.github.com/Solvely-Colin/350e74c44ec7b7324e83c131e54a295e. These screenshots are not committed to the PR branch or any OpenClaw repo branch. | Tab | Light | Dark | | --- | --- | --- | | Command | | | | Chat | | | | Agents | | | | Settings | | | ## Local iOS Demo Proof Local Mantis-style simulator proof is hosted on fork artifact commit [f07a478eadc2](https://github.com/Solvely-Colin/openclaw/tree/f07a478eadc2312f1b62f5f6cd8f7075efd335df). These files are not committed to this PR branch or any OpenClaw repo branch. - [Compressed MP4 demo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/openclaw/f07a478eadc2312f1b62f5f6cd8f7075efd335df/ios-pro-proof-share.mp4) - [Connected start frame](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/openclaw/f07a478eadc2312f1b62f5f6cd8f7075efd335df/ios-pro-proof-start.png) - [Local proof report](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/openclaw/f07a478eadc2312f1b62f5f6cd8f7075efd335df/report.md) - [Mantis-style evidence manifest](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/openclaw/f07a478eadc2312f1b62f5f6cd8f7075efd335df/mantis-evidence.json) Covered: gateway connected before recording, Settings/Command/Agents/Chat tabs, fresh chat session via `/new`, `hello` send with assistant reply, and realtime talk start/stop. Gateway proof logs showed `chat.history`, `agent.wait`, `talk.session.create`, `session.ready`, `talk.session.appendAudio`, and `session.closed` during the capture. ## Verification - `git diff --check` - `xcodebuildmcp build_sim -skipPackagePluginValidation` - `status: SUCCEEDED` - `xcodebuildmcp test_sim -skipPackagePluginValidation` - `213 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped` - `xcresult: /Users/solvely/Library/Developer/XcodeBuildMCP/workspaces/openclaw-41259b75abfe/result-bundles/test_sim_2026-05-27T17-36-31-133Z_pid12709_89151393.xcresult` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts` - `Test Files 4 passed (4)` - `Tests 56 passed (56)` - `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` - `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` - out-of-scope findings were ignored by autoreview because they are outside this branch diff ## Real Behavior Proof Behavior addressed: iOS pro UI connects through the gateway for chat, voice, session/status surfaces, and settings diagnostics. Real environment tested: Colin's iPhone installed with the iOS app, paired to OpenClaw gateway `2026.5.26`, plus iPhone 17 Pro simulator visual/build/test pass. Exact steps or command run after this patch: installed app on phone, sent a chat message, started/stopped voice, checked gateway logs, ran simulator build/test, ran focused gateway tests, captured light/dark screenshots for each tab. Evidence after fix: external light/dark screenshots are embedded above; gateway runtime proof is copied below. ```text 2026-05-27T12:02:49.605-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ agent.wait 5318ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=BA2125EC…C9A5 2026-05-27T12:02:49.705-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 75ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=91A1067B…DB58 2026-05-27T12:02:49.706-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 76ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=0485AB49…6267 2026-05-27T12:02:53.553-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ talk.session.create 221ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=DA078AF6…02C4 2026-05-27T12:02:53.885-04:00 talk turn.started session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.567-04:00 talk session.ready session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.654-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:56.562-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:57.411-04:00 talk output.text.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:00.438-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.111-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.210-04:00 talk session.closed session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true ``` Observed result after fix: chat send completed through `agent.wait`, chat history refreshed, realtime talk session was created, produced transcript/text/audio events, and closed cleanly. What was not tested: App Store/TestFlight distribution; proof is limited to local simulator validation and Colin's directly installed iPhone build.

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: test(policy): add config coverage report
target.body:
## Summary

This is the final policy-series maintainer follow-up. It adds an internal, report-only coverage inventory for the OpenClaw config fields that Policy currently watches for conformance or attestation drift.

- adds `pnpm policy:config-coverage`
- keeps the tool internal to repo maintainers, not an `openclaw policy` user command
- classifies monitored config leaf paths as observed by the policy surface
- supports `--check`, `--json`, and `--show-covered` for policy maintainer workflows

Default mode is intentionally report-only so ordinary config PRs do not have to update Policy. Policy maintainers can run `pnpm policy:config-coverage --check` when they want unclassified or stale monitored paths to fail locally.

## Stack note

This branch is currently opened against `main` for GitHub visibility, but the reviewable change is the final commit:

- `66eb73dbb19 test(policy): add config coverage report`

It is stacked after the active policy line, including #85572, #85744, #86768, #87056, and #87074.

## Verification

- `pnpm policy:config-coverage --check`
- `pnpm policy:config-coverage --check --json`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 package.json scripts/check-policy-config-coverage.ts scripts/lib/policy-config-coverage.jsonc`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/check-policy-config-coverage.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local`

Evidence after fix: external light/dark screenshots are embedded above; gateway runtime proof is copied below. ```text 2026-05-27T12:02:49.605-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ agent.wait 5318ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=BA2125EC…C9A5 2026-05-27T12:02:49.705-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 75ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=91A1067B…DB58 2026-05-27T12:02:49.706-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 76ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=0485AB49…6267 2026-05-27T12:02:53.553-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ talk.session.create 221ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=DA078AF6…02C4 2026-05-27T12:02:53.885-04:00 talk turn.started session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.567-04:00 talk session.ready session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.654-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:56.562-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:57.411-04:00 talk output.text.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:00.438-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.111-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.210-04:00 talk session.closed session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true ``` Observed result after fix: chat send completed through `agent.wait`, chat history refreshed, realtime talk session was created, produced transcript/text/audio events, and closed cleanly. target.title: Policy: add policy file comparison command
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `openclaw policy compare --baseline <file> [--policy <file>] [--json]` for policy-file-to-policy-file comparison.

This is config-level policy comparison only. It does not inspect runtime evidence, credentials, or secret values. The checked policy may be stricter than the baseline, but it must not omit or weaken baseline rules. Invalid compared policy syntax returns `policy/policy-conformance-invalid` findings with exit code 1 and JSON output when requested.

This PR also extends policy rule metadata so comparison uses the same strictness model as scoped overlays: allowlist subset, denylist superset, required booleans, ordered strings, exact lists, enum validation, provider alias normalization, and supported scope selectors.

Stack note: this is intended after the sandbox and ingress policy posture PRs, especially #85572 and #85744.

## Syntax

```bash
openclaw policy compare --baseline official.policy.jsonc
openclaw policy compare --baseline official.policy.jsonc --policy policy.jsonc --json
```

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Policy file can be compared against an organization-authored baseline policy file without inspecting runtime state, evidence, credentials, or secrets.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-policy-conformance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-policy-conformance-rebase OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts -- --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-conformance.ts
git diff --check --cached
git diff --check
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md
~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local
```

Evidence after fix: Focused policy tests passed with 270 tests across `extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts` and `extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`; extension typecheck, oxfmt, diff-check, bundled extension oxlint, docs MDX, and Codex review passed.

Observed result after fix: `codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

What was not tested: Full repository CI and broad cross-platform lanes; this draft is stacked on the updated sandbox and ingress branches and awaits GitHub CI on the PR branch.

target.title: feat(plugins): accept slot owner records
target.body:
## Summary

This PR implements the read-side normalization layer proposed in #70823: `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` can now be read as either a bare plugin id string or an object-form ownership record with an `owner` field.

What problem does this PR solve?

- Today, OpenClaw slot ownership is represented only as a bare string such as `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"`.
- That makes ownership checks brittle: every runtime, doctor, validation, install/update, and plugin trust path has to assume the raw slot value is a string.
- It also blocks the next step in #70823: recording slot ownership provenance without breaking existing configs.

What changes?

- Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord` and `PluginSlotValue` config types.
- Extends the config schema so `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` accept either `"plugin-id"` or `{ "owner": "plugin-id", ...metadata }`.
- Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)` as the shared read-side helper for slot owner normalization.
- Updates slot consumers across config normalization, validation, stale config scanning, doctor repair paths, startup plugin preloading, active runtime/status paths, context-engine resolution, memory dreaming, provider auth alias/env-var trust, uninstall cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Preserves object-form metadata when plugin ID migration changes only the `owner` field.
- Updates docs and focused regression coverage for object-form slot values.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No write-side canonicalization. OpenClaw does not start writing `{ owner, claimed_at, claimed_by_version }` records in this PR.
- No overwrite invariant or `--force` behavior. Silent overwrite prevention remains a follow-up to #70823.
- No new slot keys. This PR only covers the existing `memory` and `contextEngine` slots.
- No direct implementation of the memory role-slot architecture from #86210.

Intended outcome:

- Existing configs keep working unchanged.
- New object-form slot records are accepted everywhere relevant on read.
- Later PRs can extend this groundwork instead of reimplementing slot-owner parsing in each surface.

## Linked context

Closes #70823.

Directly related work:

- #86210: adds the multi-slot memory role architecture requested by #60572. This PR intentionally lands the smaller owner-record primitive first, or can be rebased after #86210 by extending the same helper/schema pattern across the new memory role slots.
- #83637: adds per-agent compaction/context-pruning overrides. This PR is complementary because it only changes global plugin-slot value shape and keeps context-engine/compaction semantics separate.
- #60572: user demand for composable memory slots. This PR is supporting groundwork for #60572 by creating the owner-normalization primitive that the role-slot architecture can reuse.

Supporting related PRs and issues:

- #57507: prior multi-kind plugin work showed that one plugin may legitimately participate in multiple exclusive plugin categories.
- #64192: context-engine slot normalization precedent.
- #75640: plugin install slot-selection scope precedent.
- #81512 and #81515: stale plugin reference cleanup and doctor/reporting precedent for slot references.
- #82438: memory-slot warning precedent.
- #85060: memory provider parity proof pattern for active memory plugin behavior.
- #86591: current external-PR body/proof structure used as a template for ClawSweeper-friendly real behavior proof.

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, slot readers commonly assumed `plugins.slots.<slot>` was a string. That assumption is exactly what #70823 needs to retire.

The problem is not just schema shape. If only the schema accepts object-form records, but runtime paths keep reading raw values directly, then object-form configs fail in subtle places:

- startup preloading may skip the selected plugin;
- doctor and stale-config scans may miss or misreport owners;
- context-engine resolution may fall back to `legacy`;
- memory status/tool-disable paths may misread `none`;
- provider auth alias/env-var trust may ignore a selected workspace context engine;
- plugin update can collapse or lose ownership metadata.

This PR makes the read-side contract real by routing those consumers through one shared helper.

## What Changed

- `src/config/types.plugins.ts`
  - Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Adds `PluginSlotValue = string | PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Applies the value type to `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine`.

- `src/config/zod-schema.ts`
  - Accepts string slot values and object values with required `owner`.
  - Allows additional object fields so future provenance fields can be carried without another schema turn.

- `src/plugins/slots.ts`
  - Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)`.
  - Normalizes strings and object-form owners to one scalar owner id.
  - Treats blank values as unset and `none` as the slot-disabled sentinel.
  - Uses the helper in exclusive slot switching so existing object-form ownership is recognized.

- Runtime/config consumers now normalize slot owners before comparison:
  - config normalization
  - config validation
  - stale plugin config detection
  - doctor route and context-engine compatibility checks
  - gateway startup plugin id selection
  - context-engine resolution
  - memory dreaming config resolution
  - memory status and tool-disable logic
  - provider auth alias/env-var workspace trust checks
  - uninstall cleanup
  - plugin update ID migration

- Documentation now states that slot values may be string ids or `{ owner: "<plugin-id>" }` records.

## Merge-Order Compatibility

This PR is deliberately a groundwork PR, not a competing architecture.

If this lands before #86210:

- #86210 should extend `PluginSlotsConfig`/schema with `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` using the same `PluginSlotValue` shape.
- #86210 should use `resolvePluginSlotOwner()` at new role-slot read sites instead of adding new local normalization.
- Expected rebase work should be ordinary schema/docs/test conflicts, not a design rewrite.

If #86210 lands before this:

- This branch should rebase by applying the object-form slot value support to the new memory role keys as well as existing `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- The core helper remains valid; it just gets called from more role-slot consumers.

If #83637 lands before or after this:

- The two changes should stay complementary.
- #83637 controls which agent scope supplies compaction/context-pruning settings.
- This PR controls how global plugin-slot owners are represented and normalized on read.
- Later compaction/context-engine work should consume normalized `contextEngine` ownership where it reads that slot, without changing per-agent compaction semantics.

Follow-up intended after this PR:

- Write-side canonicalization to produce object-form owner records.
- Slot overwrite invariants and explicit force/override behavior.
- Plugin-side adoption of the normalized owner abstraction.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: object-form `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` records should behave the same as bare string slot owners across config parsing, validation, doctor checks, startup planning, runtime memory/context-engine resolution, provider auth trust checks, plugin lifecycle cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 source checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`; branch `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`; head `27585fb987df8d6c68886566a6280e755a507456`; local Node/Vitest/OpenClaw repo toolchain.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files 11 passed (11)
Tests 301 passed (301)

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 80 passed (80)

OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
[base-config-schema] ok

All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check passed
tsgo core check exitCode=0
```

- Observed result after fix: object-form slot owners are accepted by schema, normalize to the same owner ids as string slots, preserve `none` behavior, participate in context-engine and memory runtime resolution, validate missing plugin references correctly, trust selected workspace context-engine plugins for provider auth only when the normalized owner matches, reset/update selected slots during uninstall/update, and preserve extra object metadata when plugin ID migration only changes `owner`.
- What was not tested: live production gateway behavior, write-side object canonicalization, overwrite enforcement, or future #86210 memory role slots. Those are out of scope for this read-side normalization PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a config/runtime parsing and normalization groundwork PR, so the proof is focused local deterministic execution rather than a live model/channel proof. No production config, gateway restart, external provider account, or live plugin registry state was mutated.
- Before evidence: #70823 documents the pre-change limitation: slot ownership was only machine-readable through direct string comparison, leaving no durable place for ownership provenance and forcing each consumer/plugin to repeat brittle raw-slot checks.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Slot normalization for string owners, object owners, blank values, and `none`.
- Config schema acceptance for object-form `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- Config validation diagnostics for missing plugin references inside object-form slot owners.
- Exclusive slot selection when the previous owner is object-form.
- Context-engine resolution for object-form `contextEngine`.
- Doctor host compatibility and route-warning paths with object-form context-engine owners.
- Memory status/tool-disable/dreaming paths with object-form `memory`.
- Provider auth alias/env-var trust checks for selected workspace context engines represented as owner records.
- Plugin update migration preserving object-form metadata while changing the `owner`.

## Compatibility and Risk

Backward compatibility:

- Existing string slot configs remain valid.
- Existing `"none"` memory-slot behavior remains valid.
- Existing default behavior remains unchanged: memory defaults to `memory-core`; context engine defaults to `legacy`.
- Existing write paths that still write strings remain valid.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- Config schema now accepts object records for existing slot values.
- Consumers that previously compared raw slot strings now compare normalized owners.
- Plugin update migration now preserves object-form slot metadata where possible.

Primary risks:

- A direct raw-slot read could remain somewhere outside the audited surfaces.
- A future #86210 rebase could add new role-slot read sites that need the same helper.
- Accepting passthrough metadata on slot records is intentionally forward-compatible, but reviewers may want to confirm the object shape is the right minimum for #70823.

Risk mitigation:

- The branch adds one shared resolver rather than multiple ad hoc conversions.
- Focused tests cover schema, normalization, runtime consumers, doctor paths, provider auth trust, status/tool behavior, and lifecycle migration.
- This PR avoids write-side canonicalization/enforcement, keeping the blast radius to read compatibility.

## Current Branch State

- Base branch: `main`
- Head branch: `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`
- Current head: `27585fb987df8d6c68886566a6280e755a507456`
- Commit: `feat(plugins): accept slot owner records`
- Worktree at draft time: clean, branch ahead of `origin/main` by 1
- Push/PR creation: not done yet

## Current Review State

Next action:

- Review this PR body draft.
- Push `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records` after approval.
- Open the PR against `openclaw/openclaw:main`.

Still waiting on:

- Schwi approval of the PR body.
- Push authorization.
- GitHub Actions after PR creation.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` overrides so each configured agent can resolve maintenance/runtime behavior from its active agent scope instead of always inheriting every value from `agents.defaults`.

This is for multi-agent installations where main, channel/proxy, local/self-hosted, and Codex/app-server agents need different compaction, pruning, timeout, thinking-level, and transcript-maintenance behavior.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related: #52751, #69988, #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, per-agent compaction settings were either rejected by schema or only partially honored. Several runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction` / `agents.defaults.contextPruning` even when the active `agents.list[]` entry had its own settings.

That weakened these setups:

- main/default agent compacts with one model/provider while another agent compacts with another
- local/self-hosted agents use local provider aliases for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit or ignore OpenClaw model overrides and keep native server-side compaction behavior
- legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys still need an explicit non-main `agentId` preserved across queued/direct compaction and maintenance paths
- queued/CLI/context-engine compaction should use `compaction.thinkingLevel`, not the active run's normal thinking level

## What Changed

- Adds strict schema/docs support for `agents.list[].compaction` and per-agent `contextPruning`.
- Deep-merges per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` blocks over `agents.defaults`, so partial overrides inherit unspecified fields.
- Resolves compaction model/provider/auth-profile/timeout/cache/pruning/reserve/thinking settings through the active session agent.
- Threads `sessionAgentId` through queued/direct compaction, CLI compaction, context-engine maintenance, Codex app-server lifecycle, embedded cache-TTL finalization, memory flush, transcript rotation, and follow-up/notice paths.
- Keeps explicit OpenClaw `compaction.model` behavior separate from Codex/app-server native runtime selection.
- Adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent scope in direct, queued, CLI, Codex lifecycle, cache-TTL, memory-flush, empty/partial merge, and compaction thinking-level propagation paths.

## Reviewer Notes

The key invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR explicitly passes `agentId` into compaction and maintenance params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: refreshed live proof for PR #83637 at head `f723ad186d7879d12c61562dd4c2dab3745b5ed3`, covering per-agent compaction override resolution for model/provider, thinking level, timeout, reserve-token floor, small-context reserve-floor capping, clean config validation, focused compaction regression coverage, and actual model/gateway requests after the patch.
